name: Cardenete from Enguídanos — circular by Víllora
name_original: Cardenete desde Enguídanos — circular por Víllora
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Enguídanos traverse a landscape characterized by a diverse interplay of rivers, gorges, and unique geological formations within the province of Cuenca, Spain. The area features the Cabriel, Mira, and Narboneta rivers, contributing to varied riverine landscapes and deep gorges like the Hoz del Río Mira. Routes often pass through olive groves, almond trees, and pine forests, offering a mix of well-paved surfaces and unpaved segments. The terrain includes views of the Embalse de Contreras and the intricate limestone formations of Las Chorreras. Best touring cycling routes around Enguídanos The most popular touring cycling route is Cardenete from Enguídanos — circular by Víllora, a 31.4 miles (50.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 57 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Contreras Dam – Cabriel Valley Viewpoint loop from Aparcamiento de emergencia Vialidad Invernal Minglanilla, a difficult 27.7 miles (44.6 km) path. This route offers views of the Embalse de Contreras and the Cabriel Valley. Local touring cyclists also love the Cabriel Valley Viewpoint – Contreras Viewpoint loop from Aparcamiento de emergencia Vialidad Invernal Minglanilla, a 46.9 miles (75.4 km) trail leading through river valleys and viewpoints, often completed in about 5 hours. Touring cycling around Enguídanos is defined by river gorges, reservoirs, and pine forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, though many routes feature challenging climbs. The routes in Enguídanos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from a review. More than 20 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Enguídanos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Enguídanos?
A: There are several touring cycling routes around Enguídanos, with komoot featuring 4 distinct options. These routes are generally challenging, offering a rewarding experience for touring cyclists.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Enguídanos?
A: Touring cycling routes in Enguídanos traverse a diverse landscape, featuring a mix of well-paved surfaces and unpaved segments. You'll encounter varied terrain including olive groves, almond trees, and pine forests, often with significant elevation changes as routes navigate river valleys and gorges.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along touring cycling routes in Enguídanos?
A: Many routes offer stunning views of the region's natural beauty. You can expect to see the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5398723" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Chorreras&lt;/a&gt;, a spectacular aquatic environment of the Cabriel River, and the vast Embalse de Contreras. The routes also provide glimpses of the Hoz del Río Mira and other riverine landscapes formed by the Cabriel, Mira, and Narboneta rivers.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Enguídanos?
A: Yes, several touring cycling routes around Enguídanos are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1087381721/cardenete-from-enguidanos-circular-by-villora" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cardenete from Enguídanos — circular by Víllora&lt;/a&gt; route offers a substantial circular journey through varied terrain. Other loop options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14708977" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Contreras Dam – Cabriel Valley Viewpoint loop from Aparcamiento de emergencia Vialidad Invernal Minglanilla&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17316579" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabriel Valley Viewpoint – Contreras Viewpoint loop from Aparcamiento de emergencia Vialidad Invernal Minglanilla&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the typical difficulty level for touring cycling routes in Enguídanos?
A: The touring cycling routes in Enguídanos are generally considered &lt;strong&gt;difficult&lt;/strong&gt;. They often feature significant elevation gain and varied terrain, making them suitable for cyclists with good fitness levels and experience.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks to explore while cycling?
A: While the focus is on natural landscapes, you can find historical points of interest. The area includes the settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6098023" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enguídanos&lt;/a&gt; itself, and you might encounter views of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5413585" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paracuellos Castle&lt;/a&gt; or the ruins of the Balneario del Salobral on some trails, offering a glimpse into the region's past.

Q: Can I find routes with views of the Embalse de Contreras?
A: Absolutely. The Embalse de Contreras is a prominent feature in the region. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14708977" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Contreras Dam – Cabriel Valley Viewpoint loop from Aparcamiento de emergencia Vialidad Invernal Minglanilla&lt;/a&gt; are specifically designed to offer stunning views of the reservoir and the surrounding Cabriel Valley.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about touring cycling in Enguídanos?
A: The touring cycling routes in Enguídanos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the stunning natural landscapes, particularly the river gorges, reservoirs, and pine forests, as well as the challenging yet rewarding climbs.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Enguídanos?
A: Enguídanos offers a rich landscape for outdoor activities. While specific seasonal advice isn't detailed, generally, spring and autumn provide the most pleasant temperatures for cycling, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential colder, wetter conditions in winter. Always check local weather forecasts before your ride.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in Enguídanos?
A: The touring cycling routes listed for Enguídanos are generally categorized as &lt;strong&gt;difficult&lt;/strong&gt; due to significant elevation and varied terrain. While the region offers diverse outdoor options, these specific touring routes might be more suitable for families with older, experienced cyclists rather than young children or beginners. It's advisable to check individual route profiles for suitability.

Q: Are there options for parking near the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, some routes indicate starting points with parking availability. For instance, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14708977" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Contreras Dam – Cabriel Valley Viewpoint loop&lt;/a&gt; begin from locations such as 'Aparcamiento de emergencia Vialidad Invernal Minglanilla', suggesting designated parking areas for access to the trails.

Q: Can I find cafes or places to eat along the touring cycling routes?
A: While the routes traverse natural landscapes, Enguídanos and nearby villages offer amenities. It's recommended to plan your stops in advance, especially on longer routes, as services might be sparse between settlements. Carrying sufficient water and snacks is always a good idea.
tips: The road between Cardenete and Enguídanos, in the Serranía Baja region of Cuenca, is one of the most beautiful stretches in this area. Although not officially designated as a "scenic road," the route is featured on hiking and cycling itineraries that highlight its stunning landscapes, rivers, gorges, mountains, and natural monuments.

A path through a beautiful area, crossing the four rivers that come together in these areas, the Cabriel, San Martín, Mira and Narboneta, all flowing into the Contreras reservoir. This trail is part of PR-CU 54 Sendero de los Cuatro Ríos, the entire route is 20 km long and it is a circular route from Enguídanos.
